description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service, defining stages build, test, release, staging, and production and setting variables for container image tags and Docker driver. In the build stage add a build job that runs a global before_script to log in to the GitLab registry, then executes docker build --pull -t $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE . and docker push $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE, and runs on all branches except tags. In the test stage add test1 and test2 jobs that each pull $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE and run it with docker run $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE echo "test1" (or "test2"), both excluded from tag pipelines. In the release stage add release-branch (runs on non‑master, non‑tag branches), release-master (runs only on master), and release-tag (runs only on tags) jobs that pull $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE, tag it to $CONTAINER_NAMED_IMAGE, $CONTAINER_RELEASE_IMAGE, or $CONTAINER_TAG_IMAGE respectively, and push the resulting image to the GitLab registry. In the staging and production stages add auto-deploy-master (only master) and auto-deploy-tags (only tags) jobs, plus manual deploy to staging and manual deploy to production jobs, each logging in to Heroku with $HEROKU_AUTH_TOKEN, pulling $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE, tagging it to the appropriate Heroku app (registry.heroku.com/$HEROKU_STAGING_APP/web or $HEROKU_PROD_APP/web), and pushing to Heroku.
